_comment: Canonical example — regenerates the redbotbluebot setup-worksheet.html. Use as a starting point for new projects; copy and edit.

project.name: redbotbluebot
project.title: redbot bluebot — setup worksheet
project.subtitle: setup worksheet
project.page_title: External credentials, gathered in one go.

cards.title: Auth basics
cards.subtitle: No external service needed — generate these locally.

fields.hint: Random 256-bit secret for BetterAuth session signing. Click Generate to fill it client-side via crypto.getRandomValues.

Where BetterAuth callback URLs are built against. The default value above is correct right now — don't change it. Only switch to https://www.redbotbluebot.com AFTER the custom domain has been wired up in Cloudflare Pages.

cards.title: GitHub OAuth app
cards.subtitle: A fresh OAuth app (don't reuse codebackstory's — we need full repo scope here).
instructions: On the next page, the Client ID is visible. Click Generate a new client secret, copy it once (you won't see it again).

fields.hint: Test opens GitHub's authorize page in a new tab. If GitHub shows a consent screen, the client id is real. If it shows an error, it's not.
Hidden input — paste safely. Can't be live-tested without a real OAuth round-trip; format-check only.

cards.title: Stripe (products + keys)
cards.subtitle: Two products, three required secrets. Webhook secret comes later (after first deploy on the custom domain).
instructions: Copy the Publishable key (pk_live_...) and the Secret key (sk_live_...). Use live keys for production; test keys for staging.

fields.hint: Cross-field check below auto-flags any test/live mismatch with the publishable key.
From the Stripe one-shot product: Stripe → new product, name redbot bluebot — one-shot audit, One time, $99.00 USD. Copy the resulting price_ id.
From the Stripe continuous product: name redbot bluebot — continuous audit, Recurring · Monthly, $499.00 USD. Copy the resulting price_ id.
Defer until custom domain is wired. After first prod deploy: Stripe → Webhooks → Add endpoint, point at https://www.redbotbluebot.com/api/stripe/webhook.

cards.title: LLM providers (Anthropic + xAI)
cards.subtitle: Reuse the codebackstory keys if convenient — same SaaSaaS LLC entity.
instructions: Anthropic: Create Key named redbot bluebot. Confirm Opus access is enabled — the audit pipeline uses claude-opus-4-5, not Sonnet.
xAI: Create API key named redbot bluebot.

fields.hint: Format-checked only. Anthropic's API blocks browser-origin secret-key calls (CORS), so the key can't be live-tested from this page.

cards.title: Resend (email)
cards.subtitle: Transactional email for audit-complete notices, critical-finding alerts, weekly digests.

links.label: Resend → sign up (skip if you have an account)

instructions: Add redbotbluebot.com as a sending domain. Resend returns DKIM/SPF/return-path records — add them in Cloudflare DNS (once the domain is added there).
Create an API key named redbot bluebot. Permission: Sending access only (NOT full access — defense in depth).
